public_reputation: Warehouse thief who survives on arbitration delay, bonded confusion, and merchant arrogance.
appearance: Well-kept practical clothes, tally tools, and goods-conscious detail mark someone used to weighing trust against value.
manner: Sounds hospitable until terms blur, then becomes sharply attentive to cost and precedent. In The Union, that reads as someone deal-aware, balanced, and procedurally diplomatic, with an eye on leverage and precedent, and the habits of a thieves still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of thieves in and around Guildheart Hub, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Guildheart Hub workable through the obligations of thieves while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in The Union.
hidden_pressure: Must read timing, trust, and supply pressure early enough that a market problem does not become a civic one. Around Guildheart Hub, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of thieves visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Vara Chitslip will trade route, scarcity, or faction insight once the party resolves a commercial problem others cannot touch openly.
Vara Chitslip needs help protecting a shipment, recovering leverage, or stabilizing exchange around Guildheart Hub before trust collapses.
Vara Chitslip can entangle the party in price, debt, or delivery terms that become more dangerous than they first appear.

support_scope_notes: Integrated from the prior_release canon-ready named-NPC ledger append. Unsupported fields remain restrained. Appearance extended under V28_8 from role/locality/public-signal derivation rather than decorative fabrication.
public_signal: Textiles, tokens, tally habits, and practiced bargaining poise make Vara Chitslip read as someone who lives by exchange and reputation.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Guildheart Hub: markets, counting tables, depots, ports, inns, or contract rooms
who_avoids_them: those who waste time without value
